description: Lac operon regulatory network derived from RegulonDB (v13), with promoter/operator (lacP, lacO), allolactose, and feedback: LacI represses lac promoter; CRP activates; allolactose relieves LacI; LacZ forms allolactose and hydrolyzes lactose.
name: Lac Operon (RegulonDB-sourced)
notes: V3 = RegulonDB + feedback. RegulonDB edges: LacI/CRP–lacZ/Y/A/lacI. Enriched with lac promoter/operator, allolactose formation (LacZ), inducer relief of LacI, LacZ hydrolysis feedback.
mermaid:
graph TD
    LACTOSE[Lactose]
    CAMP[cAMP]
    LACI[lacI]
    CRP[CRP]
    ALLO[Allolactose]
    PROM[lac promoter / lacO]
    LACZ[lacZ]
    LACY[lacY]
    LACA[lacA]
    GLU[Glucose + Galactose]
    LACTOSE -->|LacY transport| LACY
    LACY -->|intracellular| LACTOSE
    LACTOSE -->|LacZ isomerase| ALLO
    ALLO -->|relieves repression| LACI
    CAMP -->|activates| CRP
    LACI -->|represses| PROM
    CRP -->|activates| PROM
    CRP -->|activates| LACI
    PROM --> LACZ
    PROM --> LACY
    PROM --> LACA
    LACZ -->|hydrolysis| GLU
    LACZ -->|depletes| LACTOSE
    style LACTOSE fill:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    style CAMP fill:#e74c3c,color:#fff
    style LACI fill:#ffd43b,color:#000
    style CRP fill:#ffd43b,color:#000
    style ALLO fill:#74c0fc,color:#000
    style PROM fill:#51cf66,color:#000
    style LACZ fill:#74c0fc,color:#000
    style LACY fill:#74c0fc,color:#000
    style LACA fill:#74c0fc,color:#000
    style GLU fill:#b197fc,color:#fff
scientificAccuracy: RegulonDB (v13) regulator–gene edges; lac promoter/operator and allolactose feedback from literature and RegulonDB binding-site annotations.

sources.title: RegulonDB
sources.note: E. coli lac regulator–gene and promoter/operator; feedback from literature.
